title: Wires
body_text:
Wires
1	Introduction to Wires
1.1	Overview of Wires
Wires is an application on the
PaymentStream
Directâ¢ platform enabling authorized users to create and receive domestic and international wires. Wires provides the following key features:
An advanced SWIFT search function that populates the destination financial institutionâs details in an outgoing wire, reducing manual entry and improving accuracy.
2-Step Security token support, providing additional security when accessing specific features in Wires (e.g., accepting or approving wires).
Wire templates for wires that are created regularly.
Future-dated and priority wires. Additional fees apply.
Viewing and downloading a snapshot of live foreign exchange rates.
Managing foreign exchange spreads by currency classification and/or by individual currency.
Customizing pre-populated customer fees for outgoing wires.
Note:	Wires are monitored in Central 1âs Enterprise Fraud Management (EFM) system. Outgoing wires that are alerted as potential fraud are managed in EFM. See Section 14.5, Outgoing Wires Monitored in Enterprise Fraud Management (EFM), and the Enterprise Fraud Management Part for more information.
Important
:	The Wires application isnât connected to your banking system. You must settle incoming and outgoing wires to your customerâs accounts outside of the Wires application.
Related Payment Solutions Manual Parts
User Management Part
Enterprise Fraud Management Part
Central 1 Current Accounts and High Interest Savings Accounts Part
Related Operations Manual Program Parts
(available to subscribers of the Operations Manual Program)
PCMLTFA Compliance Part (BC)
PCMLTFA Compliance Part (ON)
Note:	The Operations Manual Program is available on a subscription basis. For more information about the Program, please contact Product Compliance & Design at
manuals@central1.com
or speak with your Account Executive.
1.2	System Requirements to Access Wires
To access Wires, you require:
A supported web browser (e.g., Chrome or Firefox).
Note:	Microsoft Edge isnât compatible with the Wires application.
A 2-Step security token if the user needs access to certain Wires functionality (e.g., approving outgoing wires or accepting incoming wires.
1.3	Wires Availability
Users can access Wires at any time. Wire processing is subject to cut-off times. See Section 14.7, Cut-off Times for Outgoing Wires, for more information. Incoming wires are available on business days (Monday to Friday, excluding statutory holidays).
1.4	Service Charges for Incoming and Outgoing Wires
Incoming and outgoing wires are subject to processing fees. Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario can refer to the Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule, Form 2725. All other clients, contact your Account Executive.
Additional fee considerations:
Incoming Wires
The receiving financial institution can charge the beneficiary for incoming wires. Post the charge separately from the amount of the incoming wire.
Each financial institution that processes a wire can deduct fees. Therefore, the value of an incoming wire can be less than the original wire amount.
Outgoing Wires
The sending financial institution can charge the sender for outgoing wires.
The receiving financial institution and intermediary financial institution(s), if applicable, can deduct fees from the amount of the outgoing wire. Therefore, the amount the beneficiary receives can be less than the amount sent.
Trace, recall, and amendment requests are subject to fees.
Related Information
Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule,
Form 2725
(available to Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario)
